body:
### Context

CI is not possible to progress until Dev is running on MariaDB.
Benefits are that we get CI and we have the same infrastructure across the board.

### Acceptance Criteria

Maria DB installed
orchids DB installed
orchids DB functioning for migrations and will Razor Dev
title: Infrastructure: Migrate Dev DB to MariaDB version equivalant to Prd

body:
### Context

Clearing endDate should reactivate a plant but behaviour is not fully defined or enforced.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Clearing endDate reactivates plant
- Plant becomes editable
- Previous location is NOT restored
- User must set new location
- Behaviour consistent across domains
title: Plant: Implement lifecycle reactivation workflow and invariants

body:
### Context


Taxonomy lifecycle UX must be validated once real-world usage patterns emerge.


### Acceptance Criteria


- Inactive taxonomy interaction patterns reviewed
- User confusion points identified
- UX adjustments proposed and documented
title: Architectural Assurance - Refine taxonomy lifecycle UX for inactive records

body:
### Context


Lifecycle model must be assessed for performance and maintainability as data volume grows.


### Acceptance Criteria


- Recursive query performance evaluated
- Large dataset simulation performed
- Index strategy reviewed
- Optimisation recommendations documented
title: Architectural Assurance - Validate lifecycle event model scalability

body:
### Context


Stored procedure execution patterns must be consolidated into a stable internal platform layer.


### Acceptance Criteria


- OUTPUT parameter executor implemented
- Invocation patterns standardised
- Error translation consistency verified
- Infrastructure documented
title: Architectural Assurance - Mature stored procedure execution infrastructure

body:
### Context

Improve usability of camera-based plant identification workflow.

Blocked by:

- #134 

### Acceptance Criteria


- Navigation latency acceptable
- Error messaging clear
- Workflow tested with real printed labels
- User interaction steps minimised
title: Camera / PlantTag - Refine camera-driven navigation UX

body:
### Context

Shortcut workflow must validate captured text before navigating to application.

Blocked by:

- #134 


### Acceptance Criteria


- Shortcut validates PlantTag format
- Navigation URL constructed only for valid tags
- Failure cases provide user feedback
- Workflow documented
title: Camera / PlantTag - Implement Apple Shortcut validation flow

body:
### Context

Camera scanning must support OCR inconsistencies when resolving PlantTags.

Blocked by:

- #134 


### Acceptance Criteria


- PlantTag parser tolerates missing hyphen
- Case and spacing inconsistencies handled
- Invalid checksum values rejected
- Parser logic unit tested
title: Camera / PlantTag - Implement tolerant PlantTag parsing

body:
### Context


OrchidApp must support keiki propagation as a distinct plant origin mechanism separate from split and acquisition.


### Acceptance Criteria


- New propagation data model implemented
- Parent lifecycle remains active after propagation
- Child plant provenance correctly recorded
- Multi-parent propagation not supported
- UI supports keiki creation workflow
title: Propagation & Split Evolution - Introduce keiki propagation lifecycle model

body:
### Context


A complete installation guide must be produced based on clean-room deployment validation.


### Acceptance Criteria


- Step-by-step installation guide written
- Covers OS, DB, app deployment and configuration
- Includes troubleshooting guidance
- Tested by performing second clean installation
title: Database Hardening - Author production installation documentation

body:
### Context


A second Pi will be used to simulate a first-time installation to validate deployment reproducibility and uncover hidden assumptions.


### Acceptance Criteria


- Fresh OS install used
- MariaDB + schema applied from repository only
- Application deployed via documented process
- System fully operational without manual fixes
- Installation deviations documented
title: Database Hardening - Perform clean-room deployment validation on second Raspberry Pi

body:
### Context


CI must simulate clean deployment conditions to ensure no hidden environmental assumptions exist.


### Acceptance Criteria


- CI provisions clean database instance
- CI applies schema and migrations from repository only
- CI deploys published application artefact
- Application starts successfully without manual configuration
- Smoke test endpoint validated
title: CI / DevOps / Infrastructure - Establish deployment reproducibility validation in CI

body:
### Context


Production runs on Raspberry Pi (Linux ARM).

CI must verify runtime compatibility including native dependency resolution.


### Acceptance Criteria


- CI publishes linux-arm64 build
- CI executes application startup under linux environment
- Native library load failures cause CI failure
- Runtime compatibility validation documented
title: CI / DevOps / Infrastructure - Validate linux-arm runtime compatibility in CI

body:
### Context


OrchidApp relies on deterministic schema export as a core architectural contract.

CI must validate that schema artefacts match migration output and fail if discrepancies are detected.


### Acceptance Criteria


- Schema export executed during CI run
- Exported artefacts compared against repository state
- CI fails if checksum differences detected
- CI fails if migrations modify schema without updated artefacts
- Developer workflow documentation updated
title: CI / DevOps / Infrastructure - Implement schema-as-contract enforcement in CI

body:
### Context

The current CI pipeline does not fully enforce OrchidApp architectural guarantees.

A new CI design is required to ensure that any commit passing CI can be deployed to a clean Raspberry Pi environment without manual intervention.

This includes database contract enforcement, runtime validation and deployment reproducibility.


### Acceptance Criteria

- CI builds application for linux-arm64 target
- CI validates schema export artefacts are up to date
- CI applies schema + migrations to clean database instance
- CI fails on schema drift or migration contract violations
- CI publishes deployable artefact identical to production publish
- CI performs basic runtime smoke test of application start
- CI pipeline design documented in repository
title: CI / DevOps / Infrastructure - Redesign CI pipeline for deterministic OrchidApp deployment
